description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, two-story agricultural building constructed primarily from light-colored wooden planks. It features a prominent gambrel roof clad in red metal panels, with three evenly spaced rectangular windows on one side for natural light. The front facade includes a wooden staircase leading to an upper-level door, while the lower level has large double barn doors for livestock or equipment access. The structure is elevated on sturdy wooden posts, creating an open area beneath part of the building. The overall design is functional, with minimal decorative elements, and is typical of rural or peri-urban agricultural architecture. The building's robust construction and ample ventilation make it suitable for housing cattle in an urban-edge context.
description: A two-story wooden cowshed with a red gambrel roof, side windows, and a front staircase leading to an upper door. The lower level features large barn doors and the structure is partially elevated on posts.
description_view_0: Front view showing the red gambrel roof, upper-level door accessed by a staircase, and large double barn doors below.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the staircase, upper door, and the solid wooden wall with no windows on this end.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying three rectangular windows set into the wooden wall beneath the gambrel roof.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the plain wooden wall and the sloped roof, with no doors or windows visible.
attribute_door_style: single wooden plank door (upper), double hinged barn doors (lower), all wood construction
attribute_window_presence: three rectangular windows on one side, evenly spaced
